perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Secret Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Secret Information" only in the first instance in Section 3 creates an in-text contradiction. The remainder of Section 3, and the rest of the agreement, continues to use "Confidential Information," making it unclear whether "Secret Information" is a subset of, equivalent to, or distinct from "Confidential Information." This change is most impactful on the obligations described within the section itself because there is an in-text contradiction. This is contradcting this definition because it is mentioned later on in the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Secret Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Confidential Information" to "Secret Information" in the initial mention within Section 3, while the remainder of Section 3 and the agreement continues using "Confidential Information." This creates an in-text contradiction because the document doesn't define if "Secret Information" is a subset of, equivalent to, or distinct from "Confidential Information," leading to ambiguity and conflicting obligations within the same section. The contradicted text, being the remainder of Section 3, still refers to 'Confidential Information', thereby directly conflicting with the initial altered term 'Secret Information'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Marking. Confidential Information may be disclosed either visually, orally or in writing. Written material shall be identified and labeled "Confidential" or "Proprietary" to discloser. Verbal or visual information should be identified as "Confidential Information" when disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Marking. Sensitive Information may be disclosed either visually, orally or in writing. Written material shall be identified and labeled "Confidential" or "Proprietary" to discloser. Verbal or visual information should be identified as "Confidential Information" when disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the initial mention of "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" in the paragraph on Marking introduces uncertainty. It's not clear if 'Sensitive Information' and 'Confidential Information' are the same thing or different. The rest of the section refers to Confidential Information, making it unclear how to handle Sensitive Information disclosed visually, orally or in writing. It is an in-text contradiction in this same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Marking. Sensitive Information may be disclosed either visually, orally or in writing. Written material shall be identified and labeled "Confidential" or "Proprietary" to discloser. Verbal or visual information should be identified as "Confidential Information" when disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in the marking section introduces a contradiction. The explanation states that it's unclear if these terms are the same or different, while the rest of the section continues to refer to 'Confidential Information'. This misaligned terminology, as per the definition, creates uncertainty and conflicting obligations within the same section, which is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Mediation. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Arbitration' to 'Mediation' in subsection (a) creates a direct in-text contradiction. The text following the change continues to define the process as 'arbitration' despite the heading now suggesting 'mediation.' This could lead to disputes over which dispute resolution method is actually agreed upon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Mediation. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the heading of section 8(a) from 'Arbitration' to 'Mediation' while the body of the text still refers to 'arbitration' and 'arbitrator'. This creates a direct contradiction as the heading indicates mediation, while the content describes arbitration procedures. The contradicted text, which is in the same location, highlights this conflict. The perturbation explanation states that this creates a contradiction, and the definitions provided state that Misaligned Terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. This change breaks compatibility, making enforcement unclear as it's uncertain whether the process is mediation or arbitration.
